serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cookies and cream donut weighing approximately 70 g, as typically served at commercial donut chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, glazed, enriched (FDC ID 172756); adapted to reflect white chocolate frosting and cookie topping profile
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for a cookies and cream donut exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central yeast-leavened glazed doughnut (FDC ID 172756) as the closest approved-source analogue. The input record's GrabFood-derived values (confidence 0.7) were cross-validated against this USDA entry and found to be within the plausible range for a frosted, cookie-topped deep-fried yeast donut. Calcium (42.86 mg/100g) and iron (1.14 mg/100g) values from the input record are consistent with enriched flour doughnut products in the USDA database. Saturated fat (11.43 g/100g) is elevated relative to a plain glazed doughnut, consistent with the addition of white chocolate frosting. Trans fat (0.71 g/100g) is plausible for a commercially deep-fried product.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Doughnut, yeast-leavened, glazed used as analogous estimate (GI=76, glucose reference).
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a cookies and cream frosted donut. GI of 76 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for yeast-leavened glazed doughnut (GI=76, glucose reference), published in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:12), which is the closest approved analogous food. The white chocolate frosting and cookie topping add additional sugar load but do not substantially alter the GI relative to the base dough. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (76 × 30.0 g) / 100 = 22.8. Available carbohydrate per 70 g serving = 42.86 × 0.70 = 30.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (25.71 g/100g) and sodium (257 mg/100g) combined with saturated fat (11.43 g/100g) and trans fat (0.71 g/100g) from deep-frying and white chocolate frosting
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this food: the high sugar content (25.71 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention, the sodium load (257 mg/100g; approximately 180 mg per donut) adds direct vascular pressure, and the saturated and trans fat content from deep-frying and white chocolate frosting contributes to arterial stiffness and endothelial dysfunction.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high GI (76), high sugar (25.71 g/100g), high glycemic load per serving (22.8), and minimal dietary fibre (1.43 g/100g) to moderate glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this food entirely: the GI of 76 combined with a glycemic load of 22.8 per donut and sugar content of 25.71 g/100g will cause a rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose spike, while the negligible fibre (1.43 g/100g) provides no meaningful buffering of glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (11.43 g/100g) and trans fat (0.71 g/100g) from deep-frying and white chocolate frosting, combined with high sugar and cholesterol (21.43 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food: saturated fat at 11.43 g/100g and trans fat at 0.71 g/100g from the deep-frying process and white chocolate frosting directly raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis, while the high sugar load (25.71 g/100g) contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and systemic inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); saturated fat (11.43 g/100g) and trans fat (0.71 g/100g) are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food: deep-fried foods are a hard gate for RA due to AGE formation and oxidative stress, saturated fat at 11.43 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold, and trans fat at 0.71 g/100g is strongly pro-inflammatory at any detectable amount.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Cookies and Cream Donut is a commercially prepared, deep-fried yeast-leavened doughnut sold at donut chains in Singapore. The base is a soft, airy, enriched wheat flour dough that is deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden, producing a light interior with a slightly crisp exterior. It is then topped with a sweet white chocolate frosting and finished with a generous coating of crushed chocolate sandwich cookies (such as Oreo-style biscuits), delivering a combination of creamy sweetness and crunchy texture. Per 100 g, the donut provides 400 kcal, 5 g protein, 22.86 g fat (of which 11.43 g is saturated fat and 0.71 g is trans fat), 42.86 g carbohydrate (of which 25.71 g is sugar), 1.43 g dietary fibre, and 257 mg sodium. The glycemic index is high (GI 76), and a single 70 g donut delivers a glycemic load of approximately 22.8, making it one of the most glycaemically impactful snack foods available. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its combination of high sugar, high saturated and trans fat, moderate sodium, and deep-fried preparation. It is classified as halal and vegetarian but is not suitable for vegans or those with gluten, dairy, or egg allergies. This food is best understood as an occasional indulgence and should not be consumed regularly by any population group with cardiometabolic risk factors.
